svg.title: AI Agent Platform on AWS — infrastructure architecture
svg.desc: Three-plane architecture: control plane (serverless), agent plane (stateful, private subnets), inference plane (Spot GPU plus Bedrock). Single region, two Availability Zones.
svg.text: Initial architecture · single region, two Availability Zones · Spot where interruption is free, state where it survives
Private-data subnet 10.0.64.0/20 — no route to internet
VPC ENDPOINTS — keep traffic off the internet and off the NAT meter

g.text: nested stacks, layered by rate of change

svg.text: dials out, so it needs no ingress
SPOT = interruptible, stateless, retryable. 0 INGRESS = security group has no inbound rules; administration via SSM Session Manager.
Bedrock is both the default for interactive inference and the fallback when Spot GPU capacity is unavailable — so a capacity shortfall raises the bill, not an outage.
Diagram is illustrative: glyphs and colours approximate the AWS icon set and category palette rather than reproducing them. See docs/architecture/ for the authoritative design.
